seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has been struggling with headaches for years, since adolescence
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches used to occur around the time of her menses when she was younger
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: As the patient got older, her headaches occurred both around menses and with stress
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Recently the patient's headaches have become more frequent
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Recently the patient's headaches have become more severe
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient was referred to this visit by her primary care provider because of her headaches
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches are mostly on the right side of her head
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient feels the headache pain behind her right eye
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches have a pulsing quality
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has been trying to exercise more in order to lose weight
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient cannot exercise for very long because her headaches worsen when she starts jogging
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's headaches are triggered or worsened by exercise
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has had ringing in her ears for a few months
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has recently developed intermittent blurred vision
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's blurred vision comes and goes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's blurred vision affects the side of her visual field
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient denies numbness in her hands, feet, or face
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient denies tingling in her hands, feet, or face
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient runs for exercise
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient is currently using Asics brand shoes for running
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician reviewed the patient's vital signs collected at intake
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's vital signs, including blood pressure, were normal at this visit
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician had seen a brain MRI from a few years ago for this patient
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has not had a recent brain MRI
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The primary care clinician planned to order a new brain MRI but wanted the patient to be seen urgently by this specialist first
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has not yet been contacted to schedule the new brain MRI
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: On cardiac exam, the patient has normal sinus rhythm
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: On cardiac exam, the patient has no murmurs, rubs, or gallops
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Funduscopic exam shows papilledema in the patient's eyes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Papilledema represents swelling behind the eye in this patient
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: On neurological exam, the patient's cranial nerves I through XII are intact
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has a normal corneal reflex on neurological exam
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: On musculoskeletal exam, the patient's gait is normal
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician is concerned about the patient's headaches, tinnitus, and visual changes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician states that the patient's symptoms are concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician explains that idiopathic intracranial hypertension involves increased pressure in the head without a known cause
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician notes that patients with idiopathic intracranial hypertension often have headaches worsening with activity, ringing in the ears, and changes in vision
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician is fairly confident that the patient has idiopathic intracranial hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician is concerned that continued increased intracranial pressure could cause permanent vision changes
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to obtain a brain MRI to check for other conditions contributing to the patient's symptoms
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to perform a spinal tap after the MRI to evaluate the pressure in the patient's brain
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician explains that the spinal tap may help the patient feel better by decreasing intracranial pressure
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient is worried that confirmation of idiopathic intracranial hypertension might mean she needs surgery
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician states that idiopathic intracranial hypertension does not necessarily require surgery
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician states that there are several medications that can be used to decrease intracranial pressure in idiopathic intracranial hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician explains that decreasing intracranial pressure with medication should reduce the patient's headaches
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician emphasizes that exercise and weight loss are important parts of treatment for idiopathic intracranial hypertension
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician will obtain preapproval from the patient's insurance company for the MRI
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician's front office team will work on getting the MRI preapproved
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: After MRI preapproval, the clinic will call the patient to schedule the MRI
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient
